# Screener Weekly — 2026-06-20

*Generated by the Saturday-morning routine. Source data: live `/api/screener/morning/run` against the production Railway deployment.*

## TL;DR

**API failure — no trade data available this week (eighth consecutive week).** Both the screener run endpoint (`POST /api/screener/morning/run`) and the universe endpoint (`GET /api/screener/universe`) returned HTTP 403 with the body `Host not in allowlist: trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app. Add this host to your network egress settings to allow access.` This failure has now occurred on every Saturday since 2026-04-26. The claude.ai/code routines execution environment's outbound egress does not permit access to `*.up.railway.app`. No ticker data was generated; nothing in this report should be used for Monday trade prep until the egress issue is resolved.

| Field | Value |

| Endpoint | `POST /api/screener/morning/run` |
| HTTP Status | 403 |

| Universe Endpoint | `GET /api/screener/universe?mode=swing` |
| Note | Eighth consecutive Saturday blocked — 2026-04-26, 2026-05-02, 2026-05-16, 2026-05-23, 2026-05-30, 2026-06-06, 2026-06-13, **2026-06-20** |

## Caveats / known gaps

- **API blocked (403) — eighth consecutive week:** The production Railway deployment at `https://trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app` returns `Host not in allowlist` for both screener endpoints. The message explicitly instructs: "Add this host to your network egress settings to allow access." This is an outbound egress policy restriction in the claude.ai/code routines runner, not a Railway-side firewall. The bearer token is valid — the 403 comes from the network layer before the request reaches Railway.
- Plan 1 only ships Coiled Spring — other scans (Pradeep 4%, Qullamaggie EP, Saty Triggers, Vomy, etc.) are deferred to Plan 2.
- `backfill_days_in_compression` is not yet wired into the runner — newly-detected coils start at day 1 even if they've been coiled for weeks. Plan 2 task.
- The compression proxy (rolling stddev/SMA20) is a stand-in for the real Phase Oscillator. Plan 2 will replace it.

## Root cause (unchanged from prior weeks)

The error message is unambiguous:

```
Host not in allowlist: trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app.
Add this host to your network egress settings to allow access.
```

**The restriction is on the claude.ai/code routines runner side, not on Railway.** The routines execution environment has an egress network policy that does not include `*.up.railway.app`. This must be fixed in the Claude Code web environment — the domain needs to be added to the environment's outbound network allowlist.

## Action required (escalated — eight consecutive failures)

To unblock next Saturday's run, **one of**:

1. **Add `*.up.railway.app` to the routines environment's egress allowlist** in the claude.ai/code environment network settings. This is the correct fix — the error message explicitly says "add this host to your network egress settings." See the environment network policy docs at https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web.
2. **Proxy the Railway API behind a domain already on the allowlist** (e.g., a custom domain on Vercel/Cloudflare pointing to the Railway service) — adds complexity but unblocks without needing environment config changes.
